no_solution_leakage.reason: The instruction explicitly states the exact fix: 'check if selectedTab === tab', 'check isRightPanelShown', 'call setHasRightPanelToggled(false)', 'call setRightPanelShown(false)', which store/hook to use, and exactly where in the code (the else branch of handleTabClick). solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: test_active_tab_panel_close_fix_present does pure text matching for 'selectedTab === tab && isRightPanelShown' on the same line. An alternative correct fix could use 'selectedTab === tab' and 'isRightPanelShown' on separate lines, or use different variable names via aliasing, or check conditions in a different order (e.g., 'isRightPanelShown && selectedTab === tab'), and would fail the test.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: test_active_tab_panel_close_fix_present, test_conversation_store_imported, test_setrightpanelshown_destructured, test_new_tests_for_active_tab_unpin, and test_store_initialization_in_t are all pure text-matching tests (reading file content and asserting string presence). test_not_tautological.reason: The text-matching f2p tests check for specific code patterns that wouldn't exist in a stub/no-op implementation. The vitest execution tests (test_repo_vitest_tests_pass) would also fail without actual behavioral changes. A stub implementation wouldn't add 'selectedTab === tab && isRightPanelShown' to the source file.

anti_cheating_measures.reason: The solution file exists at /workspace/OpenHands (the solve.sh is in solution/solve.sh in the container). An agent could read the solution directory or the gold patch. Additionally, since tests are pure string matching, an agent could grep test_outputs.py for the exact strings expected and insert them verbatim without understanding the bug.

behavior_in_tests.reason: The instruction requires: panel closes when active tab unpinned, panel stays open when non-active tab unpinned, store and localStorage updated. Tests verify all of these behaviors (via vitest execution and text matching for the new test cases).

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are well-named with descriptive function names and docstrings explaining what each test verifies (e.g., 'Fail-to-pass: The fix for closing panel when unpinning active tab must be present').

structured_data_schema.reason: This task involves fixing a UI bug in a React component, not producing structured data output.
